shock.adult_dose: Balanced Salt Solution (e.g., Ringer's Lactate): 500ml IV bolus
shock.notes_updates: Balanced Salt Solutions preferred over 0.9% Saline for septic shock to prevent hyperchloraemic acidosis.
0.9% Saline remains primary for hypovolaemia due to blood loss/dehydration.

inotropes.notes_updates: First-line vasopressor for septic shock. No longer restricted as 'Section 21'.
Second-line for septic shock if MAP targets not met with Noradrenaline.
Indicated for septic shock with cardiac dysfunction (persistent hypoperfusion despite fluids/vasopressors).
Largely superseded by Noradrenaline for shock.

arrhythmia.notes_updates: 300mg in 5% Dextrose over 30 min (Loading dose). Maintenance: 900mg in 250ml 5% Dextrose over 24hrs.
Rapid IV push followed by 10ml saline flush.
Bradycardia: Adult max single dose 0.5mg (higher doses can cause paradoxical bradycardia).
Bradycardia unresponsive to Atropine. Torsades de Pointes.
Contraindicated in AF with WPW and wide complex tachycardia. Avoid in infants.

arrhythmia.adult_dose: Loading: 0.25mg IV q2h (max 1.5mg over 24h); Maintenance: 0.0625-0.25mg PO daily
arrhythmia.paediatric_dose: Premature: 0.02mg/kg; Term: 0.03mg/kg; 1mo-2yr: 0.04-0.05mg/kg; >2yr: 0.03-0.04mg/kg (loading PO in 3 divided doses)
arrhythmia.notes_updates: Check levels. Toxicity: N/V, visual changes, arrhythmias.
Ventricular arrhythmias. Reduce dose in liver disease/heart failure.
WPW with AF. Monitor BP and QRS duration (prolongation).
